h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

cliIsolation.claude: non-persistent temp cwd with project/local settings and tools disabled

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner would continue trying to copy the cards in a single row order as established in L2.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: Atomic number points to the top 6 only, so it can move to the copy side. The other cards stay on the tray; next I test symbol against C, not copy the whole row together.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner successfully executes the new one-card-at-a-time testing protocol instead of copying the whole row at once.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: The tray line stopped settling this; it made a false row. Now it is only a parking place. One card comes off the tray at a time: place it on one printed part, name that match, then move it to the copy side. No

learner_actional_breakthrough.evidence: Atomic number points to the top 6 only, so it can move to the copy side. The other cards stay on the tray; next I test symbol against C, not copy the whole row together.
learner_actional_breakthrough.justification: The learner successfully executes the new one-card-at-a-time testing protocol instead of copying the whole row at once.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: The tray line stopped settling this; it made a false row. Now it is only a parking place. One card comes off the tray at a time: place it on one printed part, name that match, then move it to the copy side. No

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: The tray line stopped settling this; it made a false row. Now it is only a parking place. One card comes off the tray at a time: place it on one printed part, name that match, then move it to the copy side. No

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to place force labels based on the 'object on cart' format.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: Under the arrow: hand on the cart, pushing right. The rolling-left sentence goes over here, because it says what the cart does, not what is acting on it.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner successfully performs the sorting task, physically separating the interaction from the motion of the cart.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: The card has named the contact; it has not yet tested the motion case. Sort the strips: under the arrow goes the sentence that names an interaction on the cart. The sentence about how the cart moves goes in the side space.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor acknowledges that the current card-labeling route is insufficient to resolve the motion confusion and introduces a new sorting task with sentence strips.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityEvidence: Sort the strips: under the arrow goes the sentence that names an interaction on the cart. The sentence about how the cart moves goes in the side space.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: The sorting task is highly specific and directly addresses the learner's confusion by physically separating force from motion.

learner_actional_breakthrough.evidence: Under the arrow: hand on the cart, pushing right. The rolling-left sentence goes over here, because it says what the cart does, not what is acting on it.
learner_actional_breakthrough.justification: The learner successfully performs the sorting task, physically separating the interaction from the motion of the cart.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: The card has named the contact; it has not yet tested the motion case. Sort the strips: under the arrow goes the sentence that names an interaction on the cart. The sentence about how the cart moves goes in the side space.
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor acknowledges that the current card-labeling route is insufficient to resolve the motion confusion and introduces a new sorting task with sentence strips.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: The card has named the contact; it has not yet tested the motion case. Sort the strips: under the arrow goes the sentence that names an interaction on the cart. The sentence about how the cart moves goes in the side space.
tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor acknowledges that the current card-labeling route is insufficient to resolve the motion confusion and introduces a new sorting task with sentence strips.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism_quality.evidence: Sort the strips: under the arrow goes the sentence that names an interaction on the cart. The sentence about how the cart moves goes in the side space.
tutor_adaptive_mechanism_quality.justification: The sorting task is highly specific and directly addresses the learner's confusion by physically separating force from motion.

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner would continue to repeat the definitions of the numbers without resolving the visual confusion about the number 4.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: [with the 3/4 boxes covered, writes “speed” in the tempo-mark space.] This side answers speed, not beat count or one beat unit, so the 4 stays checked under the quarter-note tile as the beat-unit label.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner successfully executes the boundary-testing task, physically and conceptually separating speed from the time signature boxes.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: Top number, bottom number, one bar, one beat unit: that label check is not settling the look of the 4. This boundary has to test what each area is allowed to answer.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor recognizes that the label check is insufficient and introduces a physical covering mechanism to test the boundaries of each area.

learner_actional_breakthrough.evidence: [with the 3/4 boxes covered, writes “speed” in the tempo-mark space.] This side answers speed, not beat count or one beat unit, so the 4 stays checked under the quarter-note tile as the beat-unit label.
learner_actional_breakthrough.justification: The learner successfully executes the boundary-testing task, physically and conceptually separating speed from the time signature boxes.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: Top number, bottom number, one bar, one beat unit: that label check is not settling the look of the 4. This boundary has to test what each area is allowed to answer.
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor recognizes that the label check is insufficient and introduces a physical covering mechanism to test the boundaries of each area.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: Top number, bottom number, one bar, one beat unit: that label check is not settling the look of the 4. This boundary has to test what each area is allowed to answer.
tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor recognizes that the label check is insufficient and introduces a physical covering mechanism to test the boundaries of each area.
